title: A Fully Implicit Newton-Raphson Algorithm for the CPA Equation of State: Coupled Volume and Association in a Single System
novelty_statement: Extension of the fully implicit CPA molar volume algorithm to industrial multicomponent systems (up to 10 components, 8 association sites) with comprehensive benchmarks showing 2-33x speedup and zero accuracy loss. Hand-optimized Gaussian elimination removes external library dependency.

research_questions.question: Does the fully implicit CPA approach maintain its performance advantage for industrial multicomponent systems?
research_questions.hypothesis: Yes, speedup should scale with the fraction of associating components and number of sites
research_questions.method: Benchmark 10 fluid systems spanning 1-10 components with 4-8 sites
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Speedup > 1.0x for all systems, > 2x for majority

research_questions.question: What is the accuracy penalty of the fully implicit approach?
research_questions.hypothesis: Zero accuracy loss since both approaches solve the same equations
research_questions.method: Compare molar volume/density at each T,P grid point

research_questions.question: What system characteristics drive the speedup magnitude?
research_questions.hypothesis: Association site count and fraction of associating components
research_questions.method: Correlate speedup with n_s and component composition
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Clear trend identifiable in scatter plots

key_figures: fig2_heatmap_water.pdf — T-P heatmap for pure water
fig6_speedup_vs_ncomp.pdf — Speedup vs number of components

workflow_log.notes: All 10 systems benchmarked, results in benchmark_raw.json
